chunk_summary: MCT-enriched feeding and proactive fat-soluble vitamin supplementation are essential in pediatric cholestasis to support growth, prevent deficiencies, and manage clinical risks through tailored dosing and frequent monitoring.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of MCT-enriched feeding in pediatric cholestasis and its benefits for fat absorption.
Learn recommended dosing ranges for fat-soluble vitamins in cholestatic children and their clinical monitoring protocols.
Recognize the importance of trace element and mineral supplementation in severe cholestasis cases.
key_facts: Medium-chain triglycerides (MCT) are absorbed with less reliance on bile salts, aiding fat delivery in cholestasis.
Vitamin A: 5,000–25,000 units/day (water-soluble); Vitamin D3: 3,000–5,000 units/day; Vitamin E: 15–25 IU/kg/day (TPGS); Vitamin K: 2.5–5 mg orally 2–7 times per week.
Severely cholestatic infants may require monthly monitoring of fat-soluble vitamin levels and INR until stable.
Persistently abnormal vitamin levels despite high oral dosing may necessitate parenteral supplementation.
Trace elements like zinc and minerals such as calcium, phosphate, and magnesium require supplementation based on measured levels.
reference_quote: Medium-chain triglycerides (MCT) can be absorbed with less reliance on bile salts, so formulas or feeds often provide a substantial proportion of fat as MCT while still supplying essential long-chain fats to prevent essential fatty acid deficiency. (Source: NASPGHAN Position Paper)
document_key_topics: Growth and nutritional management in pediatric cholestasis
